an1.61:0.2: 7. The Chapter on the Arousing of Energy and So Forth
an1.61:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away, as the arousing of energy.
an1.61:1.2: For one who has aroused energy, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.62:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away, as great desire.
an1.62:1.2: For one with great desire, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.63:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away, as few desires.
an1.63:1.2: For one with few desires, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.64:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away, as discontent.
an1.64:1.2: For one who is discontented, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.65:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away, as contentment.
an1.65:1.2: For one who is contented, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.66:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away, as inappropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*].
an1.66:1.2: For one who attends inappropriately, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.67:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away, as appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*].
an1.67:1.2: For one who attends appropriately, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.68:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away, as lack of clear comprehension [*sampajānā*].
an1.68:1.2: For one without clear comprehension [*sampajānā*], *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.69:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away, as clear comprehension [*sampajānā*].
an1.69:1.2: For one with clear comprehension [*sampajānā*], *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.70:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away, as bad friendship.
an1.70:1.2: For one with bad friends, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* fade away."
an1.70:1.4: The seventh chapter, on the arousing of energy and so forth.
